sections.paragraph:
ῥαθυμέω 1 aor. ἐραθύμησα (X., Pla.+; OGI 521, 15; pap, LXX; Philo, Migr. Abr. 133; Jos., Ant. 14, 166), freq. ῥᾳθ-in mss. (so also Joly for Hv 1, 3, 2) but better ῥαθυμέω (PEdg 83 [=Sb 6789], 6; PHib 46, 12 [III b.c.]) to approach someth. in a light-hearted or nonchalant manner, be unconcerned, be idle, relax Hv 1, 3, 2.—DELG s.v. ῥᾶ.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Joly Joly = Hermas, Le Pasteur; s. under H—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

PEdg PEdg = CDE., Selected Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

someth. someth. = something

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 903
